name: lock-req
description: Lock human-approved requirement review candidate into authority files.
auto_start_prompt: Begin executing the loaded TODO plan now. The human explicitly approved locking by invoking /lock-req — cite that invocation as the explicit human approval for Task 1, and use the current session owner as ATLAS_APPROVED_BY. Task 1 is already in_progress — satisfy it on that basis, then drive the remaining gate commands (stamp the human-approved bundle, validate the locked bundle) to completion and produce the handoff. Do not wait for further confirmation.

tasks.activeForm: Confirming human approval before locking requirements
tasks.detail: Confirm the user/human explicitly approved the current req JSON review candidate. Do not infer approval from draft/finalize pass. Record the approver name that will be used as ATLAS_APPROVED_BY. Do not run lock commands until approval is explicit.
tasks.criteria:
Explicit human approval for the current candidate is cited
Approver name for ATLAS_APPROVED_BY is known
The candidate was finalized or otherwise reviewed
No implementation artifacts were modified

tasks.content: [REQ] Lock, validate, and hand off the approved bundle
tasks.detail:
One action, three evidence points. (1) Run the deterministic lock stamper from the existing req candidate files (lock_requirement_set.py --from-candidate); allowed only after the explicit human approval recorded in the previous todo. (2) Run check_contract_bundle.py — a failure means the lock is invalid and must be repaired before SSOT or implementation. (3) Read req/approval_manifest.json and report the locked bundle paths, requirement/contract counts, bundle hash, and that /to-ssot is the next allowed action.

Original stamper detail: Run the deterministic lock stamper from the existing req candidate files. This is allowed only after explicit human approval. It writes locked_truth.md and approval_manifest.json, and may normalize requirement status to locked.
tasks.criteria:
lock_requirement_set.py --from-candidate exits 0
req/structural_contracts.json exists and is included in the locked bundle
req/behavioral_contracts.json exists and is included in the locked bundle
req/locked_truth.md exists
req/approval_manifest.json exists
approval_manifest status is requirements_locked
approval_manifest records the real human approver
bundle hashes are recorded
check_contract_bundle.py exits 0
Manifest status is requirements_locked
Manifest hashes match disk files
Required requirements are locked/approved
requirement -> obligation -> structural/behavioral contract -> evidence_plan references close
structural_contracts.json validates generic IO/clock/reset/interface structure and references known obligations
behavioral_contracts.json validates function semantics plus cycle/timing semantics or explicit cycle waiver
req/contract_authority_report.json and req/contract_closure.json are written as derived closure reports
locked_truth.md hash matches manifest
approval_manifest.json summary (paths, counts, hash, next action) is reported
